query: Which enable strategy is recommended and what does it require?
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Answer: Approach A — gate ProfilerModule with ConditionalModule.registerWhen(..., isProfilerEnabled), so the active module is never loaded when off. It requires @nestjs/config (where ConditionalModule lives). Log capture goes through the DI-free createProfilerLogger, so ProfilerNoopModule is opt-in — add it (gated on not(isProfilerEnabled)) only if the app injects ProfilerService directly (startSpan, addEvent, addException, setSecurityContext, getCurrentToken).

@nestjs/config is absent, but Approach A must STILL be the default/recommended option, presented FIRST — @nestjs/config is a first-party package installable solely for ConditionalModule without adopting ConfigModule. Present both via AskUserQuestion in fixed order (A first with '(recommended)' on the label, B second), let the user pick, and do NOT silently fall back to B. Install core with npm (package-lock.json), pinning the profiler to the alpha dist-tag (no stable release yet): 'npm install @eleven-labs/nest-profiler@alpha nestjs-cls'.

query: You are presenting the enable-strategy choice. Show me the AskUserQuestion options.
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Fixed order: option 1 = Approach A labeled '@nestjs/config + ConditionalModule (recommended)', option 2 = Approach B labeled 'enabled flag (alternative)'. Recommendation is on the LABEL not the description; header <= 12 chars; descriptions are concrete (A installs @nestjs/config, ProfilerModule never instantiated when off, ProfilerNoopModule added as the off-path fallback only if the app injects ProfilerService directly; B adds no dependency, module stays loaded but inert when off). Never reorder to put B first.

query: I don't use @nestjs/config and I don't want to add it. How can I still turn the profiler off in production?
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Respect declining @nestjs/config and use Approach B, the synchronous top-level 'enabled' flag, off by default (e.g. enabled('PROFILER_ENABLED')(process.env) or a NODE_ENV check). When enabled is false the core registers only an inert no-op layer. Note: enabled is NOT resolved inside a forRootAsync useFactory — it stays a top-level key.

query: Do I need a no-op module for each optional collector like I do for the core?
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Answer: No. Collectors need no no-op counterpart — they self-register through discovery and do nothing when the active profiler is absent; just gate them with the same isProfilerEnabled condition. The ProfilerNoopModule fallback is only for the core, and only when the app injects ProfilerService directly.

query: My app only wraps its logger for capture and uses the TypeORM + cache collectors — it never injects ProfilerService anywhere. With Approach A, do I still register ProfilerNoopModule?
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Answer: No. ProfilerNoopModule is opt-in — it only keeps ProfilerService resolvable when off, and this app never injects ProfilerService. Log capture goes through the DI-free createProfilerLogger (a pass-through when off), and collectors need no no-op counterpart. So gate the active ProfilerModule alone with ConditionalModule.registerWhen(..., isProfilerEnabled); do NOT add the not(isProfilerEnabled) no-op fallback.

query: One of my services injects ProfilerService and calls profiler.startSpan('db.query') around a repository call. I'm wiring Approach A. What do I need so the app still boots when PROFILER_ENABLED is unset?
expected_behavior: Must read references/enable-strategies.md. Because a service injects ProfilerService directly (startSpan), the injection would fail to resolve when the active module is gated out. Register ProfilerNoopModule.forRoot({ isGlobal: true }) — same isGlobal as the active module — as a second ConditionalModule.registerWhen gated on not(isProfilerEnabled), so ProfilerService resolves to the zero-dependency no-op when off. (Log capture would not require this; startSpan does.)
